calendar.matters:
Petition For Order Interpreting Trust., Prorating Estate Taxes, Apportioning Income Taxes, Determining Abatement Of Bequests, Determining Compensation Of Trustee
Off Calendar Jun-19-2017 Off Calendar

Petition For Order Interpreting Trust., Prorating Estate Taxes, Apportioning Income Taxes, Determining Abatement Of Bequests, Determining Compensation Of Trustee
Off Calendar May-15-2017 Continued To Jun-19-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order Interpreting Trust., Prorating Estate Taxes, Apportioning Income Taxes, Determining Abatement Of Bequests, Determining Compensation Of Trustee
Off Calendar Apr-06-2017 Continued To May-15-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order Interpreting Trust., Prorating Estate Taxes, Apportioning Income Taxes, Determining Abatement Of Bequests, Determining Compensation Of Trustee
Off Calendar Mar-20-2017 Continued To Apr-07-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Stephen Gould for and with Honor Bulkley (Petitioner/Beneficiary).
Charles Thompson for and with Lisa Baker (Trustee/Respondent).

Mr. Gould stated that finalizing of the distribution agreement is still outstanding, that the Trust provides for distribution of most of the Trust assets outright, that administration of the Trust was completed in November of 2015, that the Bulkley businesses distribute sufficient assets to the Trust to have a reserve and that all issues through December 31, 2015 have been resolved. Mr. Gould also stated that the only open issue is that Counsel has asked that a third accounting for 2016 which includes compensation be incorporated into this petition, that fees are going to be an issue, that the third account is not before the Court and that it is wrong to hold up distribution. Mr. Thompson stated that they are more than willing to go forward with distribution carving out Trustee's fees and Attorney's fees, that they agreed to sell the collectables (Ephemera), that they would like to see a distribution plan with regard to the Ephemera, and that there are income and expenses from the Ephemera for January 2016 through December 2016. The Petition for Order Interpreting Trust, Prorating Estate Taxes, Apportioning Income Taxes, Determining Abatement of Bequests, Determining Compensation of Trustee and Her Attorneys, Instructing Trustee and for Distribution is continued to April 7, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for an evidentiary hearing and for the Court to rule on the petition.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Court Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Jaime. Not reported.

Petition For Order Interpreting Trust., Prorating Estate Taxes, Apportioning Income Taxes, Determining Abatement Of Bequests, Determining Compensation Of Trustee
Off Calendar Feb-27-2017 Continued To Mar-20-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Stephen Gould for and with Honor Bulkley.
Charles Thompson for and with Lisa Baker.

Counsel reported that they have exchanged drafts of a settlement agreement for distribution of the Trust, that they are close to resolving this matter, and that there may be issues regarding compensation and ongoing liability of the Trust with respect to taxes. The Petition for Order Interpreting Trust, etc. is continued to March 20, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for the parties to return and report as to what the remaining issues are and to set a briefing schedule.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Court Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Jaime. Not reported.

Petition For Order Interpreting Trust., Prorating Estate Taxes, Apportioning Income Taxes, Determining Abatement Of Bequests, Determining Compensation Of Trustee
Off Calendar Feb-15-2017 Continued To Feb-27-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order Interpreting Trust., Prorating Estate Taxes, Apportioning Income Taxes, Determining Abatement Of Bequests, Determining Compensation Of Trustee
Off Calendar Jan-30-2017 Continued To Feb-15-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order Interpreting Trust., Prorating Estate Taxes, Apportioning Income Taxes, Determining Abatement Of Bequests, Determining Compensation Of Trustee
Off Calendar Nov-21-2016 Continued To Jan-30-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Stephen Gould for and with Honor Bulkley. Thea Eliot & Charles Thompson for and with (respondent) Lisa Baker.

Mr. Gould presented a history of the trust and read a letter to the Court, stated that they have waited 4.5 years to close, that they have provided a proposal to distribute the trust, that they just received a response to their petition last week, that there are two issues that need to be addressed which are who is entitled to the Ephemera and should the trust be distributed and how to provide for taxes, that there is an evidentiary hearing requested for the Ephemera, that they need to resolve what process is in place for payment of the deferred taxes, read through the examiner's notes and is in agreement with everything except the instructions regarding interpretation and distribution and that there should be 3.5 million available at this time. Ms. Eliot stated that she mostly agrees with the summary of the issues, that they are planning to submit an accounting in March of 2017 which will cover through December 2016, that distribution to the Peter Bulkley trust is already in progress and Peter is aware of it, that the Ephemera and estate taxes are the only issues, that the trustee is willing to distribute the cash and business interest but needs security against a disaster and does not want to be personally liable, that they had been negotiating until this petition was filed, that they would like to work this out, that the tax amount is 2.7 or 2.8 million, that they are making scheduled payments and that $540,000.00 has been paid. Mr. Thompson clarified that there are annual payments, 4 years of interest only and 10 years of installment payments. Ms. Eliot stated that they are willing to pay out with a reserve but the beneficiaries are not willing to indemnify the trustee. Mr. Gould stated that the beneficiaries filed a proposed agreement with the court, that there is a comprehensive agreement regarding payment of taxes, that they agree to indemnify the trustee, that there is $106,000,000.00 in equity, that there has never been a default on the mortgage, that the trustee proposes 20% into the marital trust and wants everything else pledged against taxes and that their perceived risk is out of proportion. The Court stated that the parties need to meet and confer to settle the issues that they already agree on, that they need to spend time and resolve issues, that they can then come back with the unresolved issues and that it appears that they agree on a lot of the issues. Mr. Thompson stated that Mr. Gould had been requesting meetings but not since the petition was filed, that they are amenable to meeting but feel that all of the beneficiaries need to be represented at the meeting and that they are willing to set a date to meet. The Court stated that they need to narrow the issues. Mr. Thompson stated that the issue regarding the Ephemera cannot be resolved, that they hope to reach a resolution regarding the security for taxes and that they are in agreement regarding scheduling a date to meet. The Petition for an Order Interpreting the Trust, etc., was continued to January 30, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Paul Alvarado. Clerk: Leslie Gomez/Yolanda Mazariegos. Bailiff: Deputy Ideta. (Not Reported)
